decisions: No content promoted: reviewed daily/2026-04-21.md (1 entry), daily/2026-04-22.md (2 entries), daily/2026-04-23.md (3 entries), TODAY.md (2 entries) — all maintenance-only, no user content to extract
Retained all daily logs (04-21 through 04-23): oldest is 3 days old, first deletion candidate is 2026-04-28
Updated SUMMARY.md to add reflection-2026-04-24.md reference missing from earlier run
observations: All four memory tiers (core, semantic, episodic, procedural) remain empty after 4 days — expected since no user sessions have occurred
Daily log chain is continuous (04-21 through 04-24) with no gaps
Multiple consolidation runs per day (3 on 04-23, 2 on 04-24) with no user activity produces redundant reports with identical findings
recommendations: Consider adjusting maintenance-guard threshold to reduce consolidation frequency when brain is idle — current 24h threshold triggers redundant runs
First real user session should be monitored closely to verify the daily-log-to-tier promotion pipeline works with actual content
processImprovements: [self-critique] The consolidation process is running correctly but producing no substantive output after 4 days. Each run reviews the same empty tiers and maintenance-only logs. The process is optimized for active brains but does not adapt to idle ones — there is no short-circuit for 'nothing changed since last run'.

[proposal] Add a 'last-user-session' timestamp check to maintenance-guard.sh. If no user session has occurred since the last consolidation, skip or produce a minimal 'no-op' report instead of a full consolidation cycle. This would reduce noise in the reports directory.
[blocked] Cannot modify maintenance-guard.sh — it lives in base-brain which is readonly from this channel brain.
